original_hypothesis.title: No Fee Upper Bound Validation Allows Owner to Set Fees >= BPS Causing Permanent Claim DoS via Underflow
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: ILOManager.setPlatformFee() and setPerformanceFee() accept any uint16 value without upper bound validation. Fees are captured at project creation in _cachedProject. If fee >= 10000 BPS, the _deductFees function computes amount - mulDiv(amount, fee, BPS) where mulDiv(amount, 10001, 10000) > amount, causing an underflow in Solidity 0.7.6 (no overflow checks). This wraps to a huge value, and subsequent token transfer fails, permanently DoSing all claim() calls for the project.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: uint16 can hold values up to 65535. setPlatformFee and setPerformanceFee have no require statement bounding the value. _deductFees performs unchecked subtraction in Solidity 0.7.6.

proof_of_signal.pattern: PLATFORM_FEE = _platformFee with no upper bound check; _deductFees: amount0 - FullMath.mulDiv(amount0, feeBPS, BPS) underflows when feeBPS >= BPS

preconditions: ILOManager owner sets PLATFORM_FEE or PERFORMANCE_FEE >= 10000
A new project is initialized (capturing the fee at creation time)
Pool launches successfully and investors attempt to claim

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: No require(fee < BPS) in setters; unchecked subtraction in _deductFees
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: amount0Left = amount0 - FullMath.mulDiv(amount0, feeBPS, BPS) underflows silently in 0.7.6
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No fee bound validation in setters

analysis_questions: Confirm that Solidity 0.7.6 does not have overflow/underflow checks by default
Verify that fees are captured at project creation time, not dynamically read
Confirm that even fee == 10000 (exactly 100%) would cause issues
validation_requirements: Set fee to 10001, create project, launch pool, attempt claim, verify revert
Set fee to 10000, verify claim returns 0 tokens to user (all to feeTaker)

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Compromised ILOManager owner sets PLATFORM_FEE to 10001. New project is created, capturing this fee. After launch, investors call claim(). _deductFees computes negative result that underflows, producing a huge uint256. safeTransfer of this amount fails. All claims permanently DoSed.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Permanent lock of all investor funds for affected projects
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All investors in projects created with invalid fees
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: None for launched pools; refund may work for unlaunched pools

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: Missing boundary test for fee values at and above BPS

technical_investigation_focus.execution_timing: Fee must be set before project creation
technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Detectable by monitoring fee setter events for values >= 10000

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
